mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing equity valuations and generating positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in declining rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate crossing above 4.5 percent signals labor market deterioration and rising recession probability, triggering risk-off positioning and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in more than 60 percent of triggered instances over a five-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate collapsing consumer demand, forcing earnings downgrades and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between retail sales and housing starts loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample OLS regressions over a three-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding two percent annualized during low-inflation regimes signals robust economic activity without triggering monetary tightening, supporting short-term equity rallies.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction and produces negative returns when CPIAUCSL annualized growth exceeds three percent.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation breaching 2.8 percent triggers immediate expectations of Federal Reserve policy tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 55 percent of triggered months over a five-year backtest period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls directly increases aggregate corporate revenue expectations and consumer spending capacity, driving positive quarterly forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any consecutive 36-month window, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A widening divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales indicates weakening consumer spending relative to output, causing short-term equity drawdowns.
failure_condition: The interaction coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing over a rolling five-year horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below its twelve-month moving average signals persistent supply-side contraction and deteriorating business conditions, leading to negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive returns occur in more than 65 percent of triggered instances over a five-year backtest, invalidating the contraction signal.
